channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Japan Offers Mogami-Class Stealth Frigates to India with Co-Production Plan to Deepen Defence Ties
item.description: Japan has put forward a significant defence proposal to India, offering its advanced Mogami-class stealth frigates along with a co-production framework that would allow the warships to be built in Indian shipyards. The move marks a notable evolution in Tokyo’s defence export posture and reflects a broader strategic push to deepen military-industrial cooperation with New Delhi amid a shifting Indo-Pacific security landscape.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: SAAW to Get Satellite-Guided Variant Clearance, EO/IIR Upgrade Planned Next
item.description: India is poised to significantly enhance its precision strike capability as the Ministry of Defence (MoD) is expected to soon clear the satellite-guided variant of the Smart Anti-Airfield Weapon (SAAW). Developed by DRDO’s Research Centre Imarat (RCI), the upgraded system represents a major step forward in India’s ability to conduct long-range, high-precision strikes against critical enemy infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Russian Media hints at silence procurement plans of India for Su-57
item.description: Fresh reports emerging after confirmation from Russia’s state arms export agency Rosoboronexport indicate that multiple countries have placed orders for the Su-57 fifth-generation fighter, with Indian sources now suggesting that New Delhi may have quietly finalised an initial procurement alongside Algeria and Iran. While there has been no formal public announcement from the Indian Defence Ministry, the development aligns with earlier disclosures that negotiations for license production had already reached an advanced technical stage as of January 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Chief’s F-15EX Sortie at Nellis Sparks Debate on Washington’s lack of Understanding of India’s Security Needs
item.description: Air Chief Marshal Amar Preet Singh, Chief of the Indian Air Force, recently undertook a hands-on familiarisation sortie in the Boeing F-15EX Eagle II, one of the most advanced fourth-generation-plus fighters in the US inventory. The flight took place on April 9 at Nellis Air Force Base in Nevada, where ACM Singh flew alongside US Air Force Major Matthew Benson from the 85th Test and Evaluation Squadron.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BrahMos Joins Balikatan 2026: Philippines to Conduct Simulated Firing with US Forces
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) will showcase its most potent weapon system — the BrahMos supersonic anti-ship cruise missile — through simulation firing during the maritime strike phase of Exercise Balikatan 2026, the largest iteration of the annual US-Philippines military drills to date.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 515 Army Base Workshop Pioneering Mother-Daughter UAV Systems for Indian Army
item.description: In a significant leap towards self-reliance in defence technology, the 515 Army Base Workshop (ABW) in Bengaluru has evolved from a conventional repair and maintenance facility into a dynamic Prime Technical Incubator. This strategic shift aligns with the Indian Army’s broader modernization drive, focusing on cutting-edge unmanned aerial systems to enhance operational capabilities in challenging terrains.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NavIC “Clock Crisis” After IRNSS-1F Failure: ISRO Adopts Dual Strategy of Indigenous Development and Foreign Procurement
item.description: India’s regional navigation system, NavIC, is facing a critical inflection point following the failure of the final atomic clock onboard IRNSS-1F on March 13, 2026. With this loss, the constellation has effectively dropped below the minimum operational threshold of four fully functional satellites required to continuous and reliable Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) services. The development has triggered what can be described as a “clock crisis,” forcing ISRO to recalibrate its strategy with a pragmatic blend of indigenous capability development and immediate foreign procurement.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath to undertake 3-day visit to Germany to finalise defence roadmap
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will undertake a three-day visit to Germany beginning Tuesday to finalise a bilateral defence industrial roadmap. The roadmap will provide for a framework for the two countries to initiate co-development and co-production of various military hardware, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Monumental setback’: Congress slams govt over Pak’s role in US-Iran talks
item.description: The Congress on Monday took a swipe at the Modi government over Pakistan’s role in the US-Iran peace talks, saying Pakistan Army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir becoming a “huge favourite” of American President Donald Trump is a “monumental setback” for India which needs a complete overhaul of its diplomatic engagement strategy.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, South Korea Will Continue To Contribute Towards Peaceful And Inclusive Indo-Pacific: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Monday that India and South Korea share a common outlook for the Indo-Pacific and will continue to contribute towards a peaceful, progressive, and inclusive region, adding that both nations convey a message of peace and stability amid global tensions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Fire at Rajasthan refinery day before inauguration by PM Modi
item.description: A fire broke out at the Pachpadra refinery in Rajasthan’s Balotra district a day before its inauguration by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, officials said. Fire tenders rushed to the spot and efforts were underway to douse the flames. No immediate reports of casualties were available, they said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISIS Media Wing Steps Up India Focus, Pushes Mass Attacks And Crypto Funding For ISKP
item.description: Indian security agencies have flagged a recent spike in activity by the Islamic State of Iraq and Syria’s media network, with a sharper focus on India. While propaganda aimed at Europe and the United States has called for high-profile attacks during global events such as the FIFA World Cup, messaging directed at India is urging operatives to carry out large-scale strikes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: No decision yet for next round of negotiations with US, says Iran
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei on Monday said Tehran did not have plans yet to attend any talks with the United States. He did not rule out Iran attending talks. Authorities in Islamabad had been making preparations for another round that could potentially happen there this week.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISI’s Low-Intensity Tactics In Punjab: Shifts Focus To Arms, Targeted Killings And Drugs
item.description: Pakistan’s Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) has sharpened its focus on Punjab with a calibrated three-pronged strategy built around targeted killings, drug trafficking, and the supply of arms and ammunition, according to intelligence inputs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Manipur Shutdowns Hit Life In Both Valley, Hill Regions; 21 Arrested After Violence
item.description: Normal life in both the Imphal valley and Naga-inhabited hill areas of Manipur has been severely disrupted as shutdowns called by different organisations continued on Monday. Meanwhile, police arrested 21 people for their alleged involvement in violence during a rally in Imphal West district two days ago.
item.dc:creator: admin
